office_does: One of two state representatives for the 5th Legislative District (Issaquah, Snoqualmie, North Bend, Maple Valley, Black Diamond, Enumclaw, parts of Sammamish), serving a two-year term in the Washington State House.
race_blurb: Incumbent Democrat Lisa Callan, a House Capital Budget Committee vice-chair, faces Republican first-time candidate Patrick Peacock, a retired Army intelligence officer. With only two candidates, both advance to the November general election regardless of primary results.

candidates.summary: Republican first-time candidate with 23 years of U.S. Army service as a Human Intelligence Warrant Officer, now a Senior Industrial Security Specialist at Boeing and Vice Chair of the King County GOP.
highlights: Opposes a state income tax and wants to roll back fuel taxes he says drive up costs [S1][S2]
Would reduce regulatory barriers to increase housing construction and lower costs [S1][S2]
Supports law enforcement and accountability for repeat offenders; frames the race as a check on "one-party rule" in Olympia [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: Repeated explicit opposition to a state income tax and calls to roll back fuel taxes across pamphlet and campaign site.

climate.basis: Says high fuel taxes drive up costs and wants them rolled back — an energy-affordability-first posture, though he does not name the CCA.

housing.basis: Housing platform is entirely supply-side: reduce regulatory barriers to construction to lower costs, with no public-investment component.

safety.basis: Supports law enforcement and accountability for repeat offenders; no prevention or services component in his platform.

social.basis: Education plank calls for empowering parents and prioritizing reading, math, and practical skills.

experience.basis: First-time candidate who describes himself as "not a career politician"; military-intelligence career, no elected record.

candidates.summary: Incumbent Democratic state representative, Vice-Chair of the House Capital Budget Committee and a former Issaquah School Board director and Boeing lead engineer.
highlights: Holds budget-committee leadership: Capital Budget vice-chair plus Appropriations and K-12 Education committee seats [S1]
Priorities include public school funding, kids' mental health, workforce housing, affordable childcare, and a tax code that "doesn't squeeze working people" [S1]
Endorsed by educators, the State Labor Council, Sierra Club, Planned Parenthood, and the Alliance for Gun Responsibility [S1]

experience.basis: Multi-term incumbent with budget-committee leadership and prior school-board service, facing a first-time candidate.

taxes.basis: Wants a tax code that "doesn't squeeze working people" — a burden-shifting framing, though she names no specific new revenue source.

spending.basis: Platform leads with public investment: school funding, kids' mental health care, affordable childcare, and workforce-housing investment.

housing.basis: Emphasizes investment in "workforce housing for the families who serve our communities" rather than deregulation.

climate.basis: Pledges to protect forests and clean air; endorsed by Sierra Club and Washington Conservation Action.

social.basis: Explicitly pledges to defend reproductive freedom and supports gun-safety measures; endorsed by Planned Parenthood.
